senses.definition: A geological region or belt in Central Asia made of many ancient mountain blocks that were joined together.
examples: The Altaid orogenic belt stretches across parts of Russia, Mongolia, and China.
Scientists study the Altaid to understand how early continents formed.
Many mineral deposits are found in the Altaid region.

senses.definition: A bright white star in the constellation Aquila, one of the closest bright stars to Earth.
examples: Altair forms part of the Summer Triangle in the night sky.
Altair is only about seventeen light-years from Earth.
Through a small telescope, Altair appears as a brilliant point of light.

senses.definition: A rare mineral made of lead telluride, usually found in gold or other metal ores.
examples: Altaite often occurs together with gold in some hydrothermal veins.
Under the microscope, altaite shows a metallic shine and high density.
The geologists identified altaite as a source of tellurium in the ore sample.

senses.definition: In music, referring to very high notes played on a wind or brass instrument, above the normal highest range.
examples: The piece ends with an altaltissimo passage for the trumpet.
Clarinet players need special fingerings to control altaltissimo notes.
The altaltissimo register is difficult for beginners to produce clearly.

senses.definition: Very high notes played on a wind or brass instrument, above the normal highest range.
examples: The saxophonist struggled with the altaltissimos in the final chorus.
Some players avoid altaltissimos because they are hard to tune.
Writing many altaltissimos can make a piece unplayable for most students.

senses.definition: A cave in northern Spain famous for its prehistoric wall paintings of animals.
examples: Altamira is known for its well-preserved Paleolithic cave art.
Many tourists visit Altamira to see reproductions of the original paintings.
The discovery of Altamira changed how people understood early human art.

senses.definition: A table or raised flat surface used in religious ceremonies, especially in a Christian church, for placing holy objects or performing rituals.
examples: The priest placed the bread and wine on the altar before the service.
A large candle stood beside the altar.
They decorated the altar with flowers for the wedding.

senses.definition: Used figuratively, a thing that someone treats as more important than anything else, even at a high cost.
examples: He sacrificed his health on the altar of success.
Some people put their families on the altar of their careers.
We should not place our happiness on the altar of money.

senses.definition: Money or gifts given to a priest or church for performing religious services at an altar, especially in historical contexts.
examples: The records list the altarage paid by villagers each year.
Altarage was an important part of a medieval priest's income.
They disputed the amount of altarage owed to the local church.

senses.definition: Sums of money or gifts given to priests or churches for religious services at altars, especially in the past.
examples: The contract specified the altarages due to the cathedral chapter.
Disputes over altarages sometimes reached church courts.
The document carefully recorded all altarages and tithes.

senses.definition: Having or provided with an altar.
examples: They entered an altared chapel at the back of the cathedral.
The altared space was reserved for private prayer.
An altared room had been set aside for daily Mass.

senses.definition: A priest or person responsible for serving at an altar, especially in historical church use.
examples: The altarist prepared the vessels before the ceremony.
In some medieval churches, an altarist was paid from special funds.
The role of the altarist included caring for the sacred linens.

senses.definition: A small altar, often used in a side chapel or private place of worship.
examples: Each side chapel contained a simple altarlet.
The family placed candles on the altarlet in their home.
A marble altarlet stood in the corner of the crypt.

senses.definition: A painting or carved work of art placed behind or above an altar in a church.
examples: The altarpiece shows scenes from the life of Christ.
The museum displayed the original altarpiece from the ruined chapel.
They restored the fifteenth-century altarpiece last year.

senses.definition: Paintings or carved works of art placed behind or above altars in churches.
examples: Many medieval altarpieces were painted on wooden panels.
The exhibition brings together altarpieces from across Europe.
They researched the symbolism in several Renaissance altarpieces.

senses.definition: Tables or raised flat surfaces used in religious ceremonies, especially in Christian churches, for placing holy objects or performing rituals.
examples: The church has three side altars as well as the main one.
In ancient times, people built stone altars outdoors.
They decorated the altars with candles and flowers.

senses.definition: In relation to an altar or in the manner of something placed at an altar, especially in older religious writing.
examples: The candles were set altarwise along the east wall.
He walked altarwise toward the front of the chapel.
The choir stood altarwise during the procession.

senses.definition: A type of mount or instrument that can move in two directions, up and down and side to side, used especially for telescopes.
examples: The small telescope was placed on an altazimuth mount.
An altazimuth allows easy tracking of objects near the horizon.
The observatory installed a new altazimuth for their portable scope.

senses.definition: Mounts or instruments that move up and down and side to side, used especially for telescopes.
examples: Modern amateur telescopes often use computerized altazimuths.
Altazimuths are popular because they are simple to operate.
The company produces both equatorial mounts and altazimuths.

senses.definition: To change something, usually a little, or to make someone or something different.
examples: We had to alter our plans because of the weather.
The city has altered a lot in the last ten years.
She altered her dress so that it fit better.

senses.definition: To remove the sexual organs of an animal so that it cannot have babies.
examples: They had their dog altered at the veterinary clinic.
It is common to alter pets to control the animal population.
The shelter requires all adopted animals to be altered.

senses.definition: The degrees or ways in which something can be changed.
examples: The material's alterabilities make it useful in many products.
They studied the different alterabilities of the software settings.
Designers considered the alterabilities of each component.

senses.definition: The quality of being able to be changed.
examples: The alterability of the contract terms was limited.
Researchers tested the alterability of the fabric under heat.
High alterability can be an advantage in software design.

senses.definition: Able to be changed.
examples: The schedule is alterable if necessary.
These settings are alterable by the user.
Rules should be alterable in response to new problems.

senses.definition: The state of being capable of change.
examples: The alterableness of social customs can be seen over time.
They discussed the alterableness of human behavior.
The law's alterableness made it easier to reform.

senses.definition: In a way that can be changed.
examples: The agreement is alterably structured to allow updates.
Their relationship was alterably affected by the move.
The plan is alterably designed to respond to feedback.

senses.definition: Something that causes a change in something else.
examples: Heat can be an effective alterant of physical properties.
The chemical acted as an alterant on the material.
Language contact is a powerful alterant of grammar.

senses.definition: Causing or able to cause a change in something.
examples: Alterant forces shaped the outcome of the experiment.
They studied the alterant effects of temperature.
Several alterant factors influenced the decision.

senses.definition: Things that cause changes in other things.
examples: The researchers listed possible alterants in the system.
Social media and migration are major cultural alterants.
They controlled for external alterants during the study.

senses.definition: To change or make different, especially in a gradual or repeated way.
examples: The repeated heating began to alterate the metal.
Their policies served to alterate public opinion.
Moisture can alterate the texture of the material.

senses.definition: A change in something, often a small or planned change.
examples: Any alteration to the contract must be agreed by both sides.
There have been several alterations in the timetable.
The dress needs a slight alteration at the waist.

senses.definition: Changes in something, often small or planned changes.
examples: We made a few alterations to the original design.
The building requires structural alterations.
The shop offers free alterations on all suits.

senses.definition: Causing a slow or gentle change, especially in health or condition, in older medical use.
examples: The doctor prescribed an alterative treatment for the chronic illness.
Herbalists once recommended alterative remedies for skin diseases.
The alterative effects of the medicine were seen over several months.

senses.definition: A medicine or remedy once thought to cause a slow positive change in the body.
examples: The herbal mixture was used as an alterative.
Older medical texts list several plant-based alteratives.
He relied on an alterative rather than surgery.

senses.definition: In a way that causes a slow or gentle change, especially in older medical writing.
examples: The drug acts alteratively on the patient's system.
They believed the herbs worked alteratively over time.
The treatment was said to operate alteratively rather than suddenly.

senses.definition: Medicines or remedies once believed to cause a slow positive change in the body.
examples: Nineteenth-century doctors often prescribed herbal alteratives.
The book described several plant-based alteratives.
He collected old bottles labeled as blood purifiers and alteratives.

senses.definition: To argue loudly or angrily with someone.
examples: They began to altercate over the final decision.
Neighbors often altercate about parking spaces.
The two politicians altercated on live television.

senses.definition: Argued loudly or angrily with someone.
examples: They altercated all afternoon about the inheritance.
The two drivers altercated in the middle of the road.
He later regretted having altercated with his boss.

senses.definition: Argues loudly or angrily with someone.
examples: He often altercates with his neighbors about noise.
She never altercates in public, preferring calm talks.
The couple constantly altercates over small issues.

senses.definition: Arguing loudly or angrily with someone.
examples: Two men were altercating outside the bar.
They stood in the corridor altercating about the budget.
The altercating passengers delayed the flight.

senses.definition: A noisy or angry argument, especially in public.
examples: There was a brief altercation outside the restaurant.
The two drivers had an altercation over a parking space.
Police were called after a violent altercation in the street.

senses.definition: Noisy or angry arguments, especially in public.
examples: There have been several altercations between the neighbors.
The bar is known for frequent altercations late at night.
Police reports mentioned two minor altercations during the event.

senses.definition: Tending to argue or involve arguments and disagreements.
examples: Their discussion soon turned altercative.
He adopted an altercative tone during the meeting.
The debate grew increasingly altercative as time passed.

senses.definition: Changed in some way.
examples: He returned home to find a greatly altered city.
The altered schedule was sent to all employees.
Her expression remained calm but slightly altered.

senses.definition: The belief in or use of an alternate self or second personality.
examples: The novel explores themes of alteregoism and hidden identity.
His stage performances were an expression of his alteregoism.
Psychologists debated whether her behavior was a form of alteregoism.

senses.definition: Relating to or showing an alternate self or second personality.
examples: His alteregoistic behavior confused his friends.
The character leads an alteregoistic life as a secret agent.
Her art expresses an alteregoistic side that few people see.

senses.definition: A person or thing that changes something.
examples: The editor acted as an alterer of the original text.
He saw himself as an alterer of social norms.
In this process, heat is the main alterer of the material.

senses.definition: People or things that change something.
examples: Technology and education are major alterers of society.
The researchers identified several external alterers in the system.
Major alterers of the climate include greenhouse gases.

senses.definition: Changing something, usually a little, or making someone or something different.
examples: They are altering the design to make it safer.
Altering your diet can improve your health.
The company is altering its strategy to reach new customers.

senses.definition: States of being different or other, especially in a social or philosophical sense.
examples: The book examines various cultural alterities.
They discussed the alterities present in a diverse city.
Different alterities can exist even within a single community.

senses.definition: The state of being different or other, especially in how one group or person is seen compared to another.
examples: The concept of alterity is central to postcolonial studies.
She wrote about the alterity of marginalized communities.
Alterity can be both feared and valued in multicultural societies.

senses.definition: Belonging to another person, used especially in legal or Latin phrases.
examples: The phrase refers to the property of alterius, or another person.
In some legal maxims, alterius marks the rights of others.
They discussed the obligations regarding the goods of alterius.

senses.definition: A family name.
examples: Dr. Alterman will give a lecture this evening.
The book was written by an author named Alterman.
She married an engineer called David Alterman.

senses.definition: Someone who takes turns with another person in a position or duty, especially in older or specialized use.
examples: Each altern served at the council in rotation.
The two alterns shared the responsibilities of the office.
As an altern, he only attended every second meeting.
